title: Loops
description: Repeat work with for and while loops.
objectives: Use `for` loops to repeat a block a known number of times
Use `while` loops when you don't know how many iterations in advance
Drive a counter from a starting value until a stop condition
recap: `for` loops are the go-to when you know how many times to repeat. `while` loops run until a condition goes `false`. Both depend on a variable that *changes* each iteration — otherwise you'd loop forever.
practicePrompts: Print the sum of the first 10 positive integers.
Print only the even numbers from 1 to 20.

practiceExercises.title: Sum 1 through 10
practiceExercises.prompt: Use a loop to compute the sum of the numbers 1 through 10. Print just the final total on one line. The answer is 55.
practiceExercises.goal: Accumulate a running total across loop iterations.
practiceExercises.starterCode: // Loop from 1 to 10, keep a running total, print it
hints: Start with `let total = 0;` before the loop.
Inside the loop: `total += i;`. After the loop: `console.log(total);`.

practiceExercises.title: Evens 1 to 20
practiceExercises.prompt: Print the even numbers from 2 to 20, one per line. Output must be 2, 4, 6, ..., 20 — each on its own line.
practiceExercises.goal: Step through a range, skipping odd values.
practiceExercises.starterCode: // Print each even number from 2 to 20 on its own line
hints: You can step by 2 instead of by 1.
`for (let i = 2; i <= 20; i += 2)` visits only the evens.
Or loop 1..20 and skip odds with `if (i % 2 === 0) { ... }`.

practiceExercises.title: Countdown
practiceExercises.prompt: Starting from 5, print each number down to 1 on its own line, then print `Go!`. Output: 5, 4, 3, 2, 1, Go!
practiceExercises.goal: Use a loop that counts downward with a decrementing counter.
practiceExercises.starterCode: // Count down from 5 to 1, then print Go!
hints: You can decrement with `i--` instead of incrementing with `i++`.
